zh.(Roeux Line): 法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯洛
zh.Church of the Monastery of Saint-Feuilli: 津巴布韋米德藍茲盧曼素邱奇
zh.Rœulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France: 法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德洛利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx於1139年6月出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德洛利時，他的父親Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx34歲，母親Marie de Morlanwelz24歲。他於1165年與Beatrix de Nevele結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1186年7月31日在英國英格蘭享年47歲，埋葬在津巴布韋米德藍茲盧曼素邱奇。

zh-hans.(Roeux Line): 法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯洛
zh-hans.Church of the Monastery of Saint-Feuilli: 辛巴威米德藍茲盧曼素邱奇
zh-hans.Rœulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France: 法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德洛利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx于1139年6月出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德洛利时，他的父亲Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx34岁，母亲Marie de Morlanwelz24岁。他于1165年与Beatrix de Nevele结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1186年7月31日在英国英格兰享年47岁，埋葬在辛巴威米德藍茲盧曼素邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Eustache
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Late Greek. See Eustace .

meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

nameMeanings.name: ‘le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos Enrique, Cesar, Jose, Maria De Jesus.
Spanish (Andalusia; Alé): unexplained.
Italian (Sicily; Alé): probably a variant of Aleo .

variants.name: Aleo
meanings: From Latin. See Alexis .

variants.name: Al
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

nameMeanings.name: valet’
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English, Huguenot:  see  Vallet  .

variants.name: Vallet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Marcel, Pierre, Prosper.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French val ‘valley’ (see Val ), or a habitational name from (Le) Vallet, the name of several places mainly in the northern part of France.
French and English: occupational name for a manservant, from Old French and Middle English val(l)et ‘manservant, groom’.

en.lifeSummary: When Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx was born in June 1139, in Roeulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx, was 34 and his mother, Marie de Morlanwelz, was 24. He married Beatrix de Nevele in 1165. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 31 July 1186, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 47, and was buried in Church, Chirumanzu, Midlands, Zimbabwe.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx est né en juin 1139, à Roeulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Marie de Morlanwelz, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Beatrix de Nevele en 1165. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 31 juillet 1186, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Church, Chirumanzu, Midlands, Zimbabwe.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx im Juni 1139 in Roeulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx 34 und seine Mutter Marie de Morlanwelz 24. Er hat 1165 Beatrix de Nevele geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 31. Juli 1186 im Alter von 47 Jahren in England, United Kingdom und wurde in Church, Chirumanzu, Midlands, Simbabwe begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx è nato il nel giugno 1139 nel Roeulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Marie de Morlanwelz, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Beatrix de Nevele nel 1165. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 31 luglio 1186, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Church, Chirumanzu, Midlands, Zimbabwe.

ja.Roeulx, Hainaut, Belgique: ベルギーヘイナトローウルックス
ja.Rœulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France: フランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードローウルックス
ja.lifeSummary: Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulxが1139年6月にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードローウルックスで生まれた時，父Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulxは34歳，母Marie de Morlanwelzは24歳であった。1165年にBeatrix de Neveleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1186年7月31日にイギリスイギリスで47歳で死去し，ジンバブエミドランズチルマンズチャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx(이)가 1139년 6월 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 로우에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Marie de Morlanwelz(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1165년 Beatrix de Nevele(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1186년 7월 31일 영국 잉글랜드에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 짐바브웨 미들랜드즈 치럼아주 처르치에 묻혔다.

mn.JUN 1139–31 JUL 1186: 1139 оны Зургаадугаар сар–1186 оны Долоодугаар сарын 31

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx nasceu em junho de 1139, em Roeulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Marie de Morlanwelz, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Beatrix de Nevele em 1165. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 31 de julho de 1186, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Church, Chirumanzu, Midlands, Zimbábue.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx родился июнь 1139 г. в местности роеулкс, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Marie de Morlanwelz, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrix de Nevele в 1165 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 31 июля 1186 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности чурч, чируманзу, мидландс, Зимбабве.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eustache II ‘le valet’ du Rœulx nació en junio de 1139, en Roeulx, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Eustache Dit Le Viel du Roeulx, Seigneur du Rœulx, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Marie de Morlanwelz, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Beatrix de Nevele en 1165. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 31 de julio de 1186, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultado en Church, Chirumanzu, Midlands, Zimbabue.
